themes.title: Far-right parties surge in European polls
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties across Europe are seeing a significant rise in polling numbers, with the AfD in Germany leading the CDU by five points, and similar trends observed in France, Spain, and Italy. This cross-national pattern reflects growing voter dissatisfaction with mainstream parties and a shift towards nationalist and anti-establishment platforms.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: Leads German polls with 27% support, surpassing CDU/CSU (22%) and marking a historic high.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Journalists and analysts predict a strong chance of winning the 2027 French presidential election, with Marine Le Pen and Jordan Bardella leading the party.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Gains traction in regional coalitions with PP, pushing 'national priority' policies in Aragón and Extremadura.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Faces internal divisions but remains influential in Italy's right-wing coalition, with debates over EU fiscal rules and migration policies.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: Continues to shape Swedish politics, influencing policy debates on immigration and law enforcement.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: Gains visibility in Norway with debates on immigration and energy policies, positioning itself as a defender of national interests.

themes.title: PP-Vox coalitions spark constitutional debates
themes.blurb: The formation of coalitions between Spain's Partido Popular (PP) and the far-right Vox party in regions like Aragón and Extremadura has ignited debates over constitutional compliance and the prioritization of national policies. These coalitions are testing the limits of Spain's legal framework and sparking cross-national discussions about the role of far-right parties in governance.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Demands 'national priority' policies in regional governments, clashing with PP over interpretations of legality and constitutional compliance.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Partido Popular
countries.note: Faces internal divisions over Vox's demands, with some leaders resisting far-right policies while others seek compromise.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Monitors PP-Vox coalitions as a potential model for future alliances in France, with debates over constitutional limits.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Engages in similar debates over EU fiscal rules and migration policies, reflecting broader constitutional tensions within Italy's right-wing coalition.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Criticizes PP-Vox coalitions as unconstitutional, framing them as a threat to democratic norms and national unity.

themes.title: Orbán’s resignation reshapes Hungarian politics
themes.blurb: Viktor Orbán's unexpected resignation as Hungary's prime minister and his decision not to take his parliamentary seat have triggered a major political realignment. The rise of Péter Magyar's Tisza Party and the reorganization of Fidesz signal a new era in Hungarian politics, with cross-national implications for far-right movements in Europe.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Orbán's resignation and Fidesz's reorganization dominate political discourse, with debates over the party's future direction and leadership.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Monitors Orbán's resignation as a potential precedent for leadership changes within Poland's ruling party.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: Analyzes Orbán's exit as a cautionary tale for far-right parties, with discussions on maintaining unity and relevance.

countries.name: 🇪🇺 EU Relations
countries.note: Debates Hungary's future relationship with the EU, particularly regarding frozen funds and compliance with EU norms.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Views Orbán's resignation as a shift in Europe's far-right landscape, with potential lessons for France's own political dynamics.

themes.title: Trump’s White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting dominates global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: The attempted assassination of Donald Trump at the White House Correspondents' Dinner has become a defining moment in global right-wing media, with intense coverage across multiple countries. The incident has sparked debates over security, political rhetoric, and the rise of political violence, resonating strongly with far-right audiences worldwide.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: The shooting dominates U.S. right-wing media, with calls for enhanced security and condemnations of political rhetoric.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Covers the incident as a symbol of rising political violence, with debates over security measures and media responsibility.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Frames the shooting as evidence of deepening political divisions, with discussions on security and far-right narratives.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Uses the incident to criticize mainstream media and political elites, calling for stricter security measures.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Condemns the attack as an assault on democracy, linking it to broader debates over political violence and media bias.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: Highlights the shooting as a failure of security and political discourse, with calls for stronger protections for leaders.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Covers the incident as part of a broader narrative on political instability and the need for strong leadership.

themes.title: Far-right rhetoric fuels political violence
themes.blurb: Far-right rhetoric and narratives are increasingly being linked to incidents of political violence across multiple countries. From Italy to the U.S., right-wing media and politicians are amplifying concerns about the radicalization of supporters, while also using these incidents to justify stricter security measures and crackdowns on dissent.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Debates over political violence escalate after incidents at rallies and protests, with calls for stricter security measures.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Faces accusations of inciting violence through its rhetoric, with clashes at rallies and protests sparking legal and political debates.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Criticized for extremist rhetoric among candidates, with incidents of violence and threats linked to its campaigns.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: The WHCD shooting and other incidents are framed as evidence of rising political violence, with calls for enhanced security and media accountability.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Debates over political violence and security measures intensify, with far-right rhetoric linked to rising tensions.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Criticizes opposition rhetoric as fueling political instability, with calls for stronger security measures and unity.
